name: New York
lifestyle: Global financial capital with unmatched cultural density, but NY state plus NYC stack some of the highest combined income and capital gains taxes in the US. Manhattan housing is compressed and doorman buildings push the top tier higher. Plan domicile carefully — NY aggressively audits departing high-earners.

true_fat_fire.description: UES/Tribeca/West Village 2-3BR condo with doorman, Michelin-tier dining monthly, weekly housekeeping, 3+ international trips plus Hamptons summers.

guide.groceries: Whole Foods, Citarella, Union Market, and greenmarket-style shopping with premium produce, seafood, wine, and no real pressure to economize at home.
guide.dining: Several polished dinners out most weeks plus tasting-menu or power-dining nights, with bigger dinners often around $200-$400 per person before wine.
guide.rhythm: Mostly black car or easy cab use, weekly housekeeping, premium wellness and culture spend, and a lifestyle that assumes Hamptons, ski, and long-haul travel.

comfortable_expat.description: Brooklyn/LIC/UWS 1BR rental, dining out 3-4 nights/week, subway plus Ubers, 1-2 international trips plus Northeast weekends.

guide.groceries: A comfortable mix of Trader Joe's, Whole Foods top-ups, neighborhood produce, and better proteins for a kitchen that still feels New York-rich.
guide.dining: Dining out several nights a week plus the occasional nicer dinner, usually around $30-$65 casually and $130-$250 per person on a more serious night.
guide.rhythm: Mostly subway plus Uber, regular salon or fitness spend, and weekends aimed at upstate, the beach, or short Northeast escapes.

luxury_family.description: UES classic 6 or brownstone, top private school access (Dalton/Trinity/Spence-tier) when dependents apply, part-time household help, international and Hamptons/ski trips annually.

guide.groceries: Large family grocery basket through Whole Foods, gourmet grocers, school-lunch provisioning, and premium pantry or entertaining extras.
guide.dining: Family brunches and dinners out most weekends plus adult meals, with casual family meals often $40-$80 per person and special dinners far above that.
guide.rhythm: Part-time household help, dense school and activity logistics, and a yearly rhythm split across city life, Hamptons or ski time, and long-haul family travel.
